meaning: vt.用作祭祀,献给,使神圣adj.被献给神的,神圣的
examples: Consecrate yourselves and come to the sacrifice with me.
Now, who is willing to consecrate himself today to the Lord?
For seven days make atonement for the altar and consecrate it.
The battalions' old colours will be used for the last time at the event in Worcester ahead of a service to consecrate the new colours.
David Dinkebogile led Saturday's ceremony and stressed that the gathering was to consecrate a bishop "not a black woman, not an African, not a Swazi woman".
